name: Maria
findings: Machines have no structured 'ID card' for your business
FAQPage schema (highest-impact type for AI answers)
All major AI engines allowed to read your site
Open to all 10 AI ecosystems we check
Not found (a 20-minute nice-to-have, not a crisis)
12 headings found, none phrased as customer questions
No prices or pricing page detected
Is your Google Business Profile claimed, 100% complete, and updated in the last 30 days?
It feeds Google's AI answers directly; inactive profiles lose impressions.
Have you received (and responded to) reviews in the last 30 days?
Volume, recency, and responses are core AI trust signals.
Have you claimed Bing Places AND Apple Business (formerly Business Connect)?
Bing Places feeds Microsoft Copilot and Alexa; Apple Business is the ONLY source Siri checks.
Is your business listed with current info on Yelp (or your industry's main review site)?
Alexa+ pulls local business data primarily from Yelp.

fixes.f: Add Organization or LocalBusiness JSON-LD with name, description, contact info, and a sameAs array linking every profile (Google, LinkedIn, socials, Yelp).

fixes.t: Is your Google Business Profile claimed, 100% complete, and updated in the last 30 days?
fixes.f: Claim and complete your Google Business Profile and post 1–2× per week — it feeds Gemini-powered answers directly.

fixes.t: FAQPage schema (highest-impact type for AI answers)
fixes.f: Build a FAQ page from your 10 most-asked customer questions and mark it up with FAQPage schema — the highest-impact schema for AI answers.
Publish prices or honest ranges. “Contact us for pricing” means the AI quotes your competitor's transparent page instead — and the agent books them.

fixes.t: Have you claimed Bing Places AND Apple Business (formerly Business Connect)?
fixes.f: Claim both free profiles: bingplaces.com (feeds Copilot + Alexa + ChatGPT's ecosystem) and business.apple.com (the only data source Siri and Apple Intelligence check for local businesses).
